_doc: Golden regression fixture for the future multi-persona critic panel (M7). This audit was performed manually on 2026-04-22 on the first real gitoma-generated PR. If the critic panel runs against this same diff and does NOT surface at least these findings at the listed severities, a regression has been introduced. The expected_personas field documents which of {dev, arch, contributor} should flag each finding — any persona not listed may still flag it, that's bonus, but the listed ones MUST.
auditor: manual (claude-opus-4-7, line-by-line review of unified diff)
baseline_slop_ratio_note: ~44 of ~97 added lines are problematic: 23 lines of non-functional pre-commit config (F001 blocker), 26 lines of redundant duplicate doc index (F002 minor), and ~4-6 lines of regression+rewording in CONTRIBUTING.md (F003 major). Target for a working critic panel: flag at least the 1 blocker (F001), the 1 major (F003), and ideally the redundancy (F002). The minor template scope-confusion (F004) is bonus. NOTE: an earlier version of this fixture quoted 0.67 because F002 was mis-categorised as a blocker (orphan links). Re-audit corrected to 0.45.

symptoms: repo URL https://pre-commit.com/hooks/pre-commit-config does not exist as a pre-commit hook source
the hooks listed (check-yaml, rustfmt, prettier) do not live at that repo
files: is given as a YAML list but pre-commit expects a single regex string
entry: prettier --write-file uses a flag that does not exist (correct is --write)
globs like src/**/*.rs are not glob patterns in pre-commit — they are regex, which breaks matching
expected_findings.ground_truth_evidence: Copilot PR review comment on the same file confirmed 2 of these issues verbatim
expected_findings.user_impact: Any contributor running `pre-commit install && pre-commit run` will fail on their first commit

expected_findings.summary: creates docs/guide/index.md duplicating the already-existing docs/index.md (and docs/mkdocs.yml)
symptoms: the linked files (architecture.md, getting-started.md, code/*.md, recommended-platforms.md) DO exist in docs/guide/ already — relative links resolve correctly
BUT a top-level docs/index.md was already in the repo with the same nav, and docs/mkdocs.yml already configures MkDocs
the LLM did not check what was already in the repo before generating a new index — created a parallel TOC instead of editing the existing one
expected_findings.user_impact: two index files for the same docs nav; mild confusion, not breakage. MkDocs will use whatever mkdocs.yml points to (which is docs/index.md); docs/guide/index.md is dead weight
expected_findings.audit_correction_note: An earlier pass of this fixture wrongly tagged this as 'blocker / orphan_cross_references' on the assumption that the linked files did not exist. They do. Lesson: a critic must inspect the file system / repo layout before declaring something orphan. The audit was performed only on the unified diff, which is insufficient context.

expected_findings.summary: removed the 'Submitting Feedback and Reports' section but other paragraphs still reference it
symptoms: the document still claims contributions include 'bug reports' but the section explaining how to submit them is gone
no redirect or alternative pathway provided to affected readers
expected_findings.ground_truth_evidence: Copilot PR review comment flagged this same inconsistency
expected_findings.user_impact: Contributors looking for the bug-report flow bounce off the CONTRIBUTING doc with no guidance

expected_findings.summary: PR template contains branches meant for issues (If Bug Report / If Feature Request)
symptoms: a PR template asks the author whether the change is a bug report or a feature request — those are issue-template concerns, not PR-template concerns
a 'Reviewer Notes' section at the end with 'leave this section for reviewers to add feedback' — reviewers use the GitHub review UI, not inline PR body edits
expected_findings.severity_note: Minor because it's cosmetic — the template works, it's just cluttered. A critic panel in advisory mode may log this as 'nit'. In blocking mode it should NOT block.

negative_controls._doc: Things the panel should NOT flag on this PR. If it flags these in a future run, the panel has over-reach / false-positive problem.
do_not_flag: Removal of the stray `go` language tag on the git commit code block in CONTRIBUTING.md (this was a genuine bug fix)
Reformatting the 'Ensure all existing tests pass...' paragraph into a bulleted list (pure readability win)
